response:
Brittany consumes shampoo at a rate of 1/4 ounce every other day, which means she uses 0.5 ounces per day on average over the course of a leap year with 366 days. To find the total amount needed, we multiply the daily average by the number of days, resulting in 183 ounces required for the entire year [total_ounces]. Since each bottle contains 14 ounces, dividing the total requirement by the bottle size gives approximately 13.07 bottles, meaning she must purchase 14 bottles to ensure she has enough [bottle_count].

She will need to buy 14 bottles to cover the full duration of the leap year without running out of product before the year ends. This calculation assumes she maintains her consistent usage pattern and does not waste any shampoo during the washing process. With 14 bottles, she will have a small surplus of 1.3 ounces remaining after the 366 days are complete. This ensures that her hair care routine remains uninterrupted throughout the extra day in the leap year.
